sourceText: A king must be a lion in battle and a fox in council.
Leadership requires both overwhelming force and cunning strategy. One must be bold on the battlefield and wise in politics.
Destiny may place you in chains, but it is your will that forges the key.
One's origins do not define their ultimate potential; personal drive is paramount.
A bridge built is a stronger defense than a wall.
Connecting your empire through trade and communication builds more lasting strength than simply isolating it.

Forced from his home by the Mongol invasion, he spent 30 years as a wanderer across Anatolia, Syria, Egypt, and Iraq, living an unstable life. He faced numerous hardships, including being taken prisoner by Crusaders.

He turned his vast travel experiences and observations of diverse people into his greatest literary assets. Upon returning to Shiraz, he distilled these profound insights into his masterpieces, 'Gulistan' and 'Bustan', which contain universal wisdom for humanity.
The children of Adam are limbs of one body, having been created of one essence.
This expresses the spirit of humanism, that all of humanity is fundamentally connected as one, and the suffering of one is the suffering of all.
Have patience. All things are difficult before they become easy.
This advises perseverance and resilience, reminding us that initial struggles are a natural part of any worthwhile endeavor.
A little and a little, collected, make a great deal.
